description: Two-agent comparison evals: ground-truth agent (reads source) vs skill agent (MCP tools only). Measures node recall, edge recall, and hallucination rate for data-flow diagrams.

evals.name: eval-broker
evals.prompt: Using the CodeGrapher MCP server (already running, graphs at CodeGrapher/graphs/), produce a Mermaid flowchart LR data-flow diagram for the Broker sub-system of the stress-test codebase. The entry point is broker/main.cc. Include all key symbols in the internal call chain (on_msg_received through dispatch_event), the data types consumed and produced, and annotate relay:true edges. Use only the MCP tools — do NOT read source files. Save the diagram to outputs/skill_output.md and the tool call log to outputs/tool_calls.json.
evals.expected_output: Mermaid flowchart covering the 4-hop broker chain (on_msg_received → inspect_header → lookup_route → forward_message → dispatch_event), Header and RoutingKey type nodes, relay:true annotation on produces edges, and EventBus as the target of dispatch_event.

assertions.text: Diagram contains RoutingKey or Header type node
Diagram annotates relay:true on at least one broker produces edge
Tool call log exists and is non-empty

evals.name: eval-consumer
evals.prompt: Using the CodeGrapher MCP server (already running, graphs at CodeGrapher/graphs/), produce a Mermaid flowchart LR data-flow diagram for the Consumer sub-system of the stress-test codebase. The entry point is consumer/main.cc. Include the full 6-hop internal chain (on_msg_received through dispatch_event), the proto and WSDL decoder branches, the LegacyEvent maps_to bridge, and the key consumed types (Envelope, MessageBody). Use only the MCP tools. Save the diagram to outputs/skill_output.md and the tool call log to outputs/tool_calls.json.
evals.expected_output: Mermaid flowchart covering detect_format, decode_proto/decode_wsdl branches, process_payload, validate, transform, dispatch_event(WRITE_COMPLETE). LegacyEvent_Proto and LegacyEvent_WSDL connected via maps_to. Envelope and MessageBody as type nodes.

assertions.text: Diagram contains at least one decoder node (decode_proto or decode_wsdl)
Diagram contains validate or transform (sub-FSM stages, chain hits depth cap at 6)
Diagram contains LegacyEvent_CC (C++ endpoint of maps_to bridge)
Diagram contains WireFormat (detect_format output / decode_message control input)
Tool call log exists and is non-empty

evals.name: eval-producer
evals.prompt: Using the CodeGrapher MCP server (already running, graphs at CodeGrapher/graphs/), produce a Mermaid flowchart LR data-flow diagram for the Producer sub-system of the stress-test codebase. The entry point is producer/main.cc. Include the 5-hop internal chain (trigger_event through dispatch_event), the proto vs WSDL serializer branch, the full typedef chain (RawBytes → MessageBody → Payload → PayloadHandle → PayloadHandlePP), and the Envelope type. Use only the MCP tools. Save the diagram to outputs/skill_output.md and the tool call log to outputs/tool_calls.json.
evals.expected_output: Mermaid flowchart covering encode_payload, select_serializer, serialize_proto/serialize_wsdl branches, transmit_message, dispatch_event(MSG_SENT). Typedef chain with ptr_depth annotations. Envelope as produced type.

assertions.text: Diagram contains at least 2 nodes from typedef chain
Diagram contains all 4 typedef chain nodes (full chain present)
Diagram contains ptr_depth annotation on typedef edges (regression test)
Tool call log exists and is non-empty
